謹賀新年.meaning: hanja form of 근하신년 (“Happy New Year”)

百聞不如一見.meaning: hanja form of 백문불여일견 (“seeing is believing”)

새해 복 많이 받으세요.meaning: an expression wishing one the best in a new year; Happy New Year

모르는 것이 약이다.meaning: "not knowing is the cure/medicine"; ignorance is bliss

아는 것이 탈.meaning: folly to be wise, ignorance is bliss.

호미로 막을 것을 가래로 막는다.meaning: a stitch in time saves nine.

빈 수레가 요란하다.meaning: empty vessels make the most sound, that is, "noisy, opinionated people are often stupid."

세월은 사람을 기다려 주지 않는다.meaning: time and tide wait for no man

22.meaning: That applies to me as well.

갑과 을.meaning: A and B, former and latter

안녕히 계세요.meaning: goodbye (said by the person leaving to the person staying)

안녕히 가세요.meaning: goodbye (said by the person staying to the person leaving)

솔까말.meaning: frankly speaking, speaking honestly, to be honest (TBH)

누워서 떡 먹기.meaning: an easily accomplished task; a piece of cake; a walk in the park

콩 심은 데 콩 나고 팥 심은 데 팥 난다.meaning: As you sow, so shall you reap.

국제 음성 기호.meaning: International Phonetic Alphabet (a standardized set of symbols for representing the sounds of human speech)

달걀 한 판.meaning: synonym of 계란 한 판 (gyeran han pan, “(figurative) 30 years of age”)

신종 코로나 감염증.meaning: short for 신종 코로나바이러스 감염증 (sinjong koronabaireoseu gamyeomjeung)

꼬리 치다.meaning: to wag the tail (for an animal)

말이 아니다.meaning: to be unreasonable; to be ridiculous

말이 씨가 된다.meaning: Watch what you say; be careful of what you say; if you say something bad, it might come true

다름이 아니라.meaning: Used to initiate a formal and highly polite conversation

말을 놓다.meaning: to forgo linguistic formality distinctions in conversation, a sign of intimacy

잘 먹겠습니다.meaning: I/we will eat; let's eat (a stock phrase used before starting a meal)

먼 무당이 용하다.meaning: the grass is always greener on the other side

저리 가라.meaning: enough with, out with, it's over for (Used to refer to something which has just become obsolete or been done away with d

지성이면 감천이다.meaning: sincerity moves heaven; if one is truly sincere about achieving something, one shall attain it

장고 끝에 악수 둔다.meaning: you should trust your gut feeling

개가 짖어도 기차는 달린다.meaning: the dogs bark, but the caravan goes on

없는 자리에서는 나라님 욕도 한다.meaning: People like to talk behind the backs of others; people like to speak badly of others

어시 없이는 살아두 쉐 없이는 못 산다.meaning: A proverb about the importance of cattle in traditional Korean life.

밑져야 본전이다.meaning: to have nothing to lose (said when beginning a new enterprise, etc.)

이 뭣고.meaning: "What is this?"; one of the most popular questions (or koans) meditated on in contemporary Korean Buddhism

시심마.meaning: synonym of 이 뭣고 (i mwotgo, “What is this”, a Zen Buddhist koan)

아이와 늙은이는 괴는 데로 간다.meaning: The children and the old [alike] will go to where they are loved; (alternatively) the children and the old [alike] will

온실 속의 화초.meaning: "flower in a greenhouse"; an excessively sheltered person who cannot cope with the real world

한국말은 끝까지 들어봐야 한다.meaning: "In the Korean language, one must listen until the end"; one should listen to everything that others have to say before

아이가.meaning: A phrase used to form tag questions, inviting the listener to agree.

선무당이 사람 잡는다.meaning: "the novice shaman kills the person [that they were trying to heal]"; a little knowledge is a dangerous thing

니애미.meaning: fuck, fuck you; a highly profane expression, often used as an insult

이상 무.meaning: no abnormalities, all clear, everything is going well and normally

죽다 살았다.meaning: to have come out safely from a painful or risky situation

반풍수가 집안 망친다.meaning: "half-learned feng shui brings the household to ruin"; a little knowledge is a dangerous thing

벼는 익을수록 고개를 숙인다.meaning: "the riper the rice plant, the more it bends its head":

곳간에서 인심 난다.meaning: "generosity is born from the storehouse": one must be wealthy to afford to be generous towards others; only the wealthy

기차 떠나고 손 흔들다.meaning: "to wave after the train has left"; to be affected by l'esprit de l'escalier

밥 뭇나.meaning: what's up; a casual greeting between friends

제곧내.meaning: Used as a filler for the content of a post when the title already says everything one wants to say.

식은 죽 먹기.meaning: doing something easily and without hesitation; taking candy from a baby; a piece of cake; a cakewalk

엎친 데 덮치다.meaning: to make matters worse/add insult to injury; for one bad event/misfortune to follow another; it never rains but it pours

잠을 청하다.meaning: to try and fall asleep; to lull oneself to sleep

시도 때도 없이.meaning: adverbial form of 시(時)도 때도 없다 (sido ttaedo eopda, “to be recurrent”): recurrently

한도 끝도 없다.meaning: to be endless; to be limitless; to be uncountable

까마귀 고기를 먹었나.meaning: Used to criticize or tease someone for forgetting something.

할 것 없다.meaning: to have no need to distinguish; to be without distinction

사공이 많으면 배가 산으로 간다.meaning: "if there are many captains, the ship goes to the mountains"; too many cooks spoil the broth

술이 떡이 되다.meaning: "alcohol becomes tteok": to be dead drunk

호랑이는 죽어서 가죽을 남기고 사람은 죽어서 이름을 남긴다.meaning: a person should focus on the legacy they will have on others

호랑이 잡아먹는 담비.meaning: "a marten that hunts tigers"; a David before a Goliath

라면 먹고 갈래.meaning: "Do you want to have instant noodle [at my place] before you leave?"; do you want to Netflix and chill?

whyrano.meaning: What's going on? What's wrong? Why is it like this?

쇠뿔도 단김에 빼랬다.meaning: strike while the iron is hot

교하질쉬.meaning: "scolding the village head under the bridge": complaining behind someone's back

예나 지금이나.meaning: whether in old times or nowadays; used when a state or quality in the past has not been changed and still remains today

광에서 인심 난다.meaning: alternative form of 곳간에서 인심 난다 (gotganeseo insim nanda, “only the wealthy are generous”)

별이 빛나는 밤.meaning: The Starry Night (a painting by Vincent van Gogh)

하늘의 별 따기.meaning: get blood out of a stone (something that is almost impossible)

갗에서 좀 난다.meaning: the root of the issue (or problem) can be found within itself

흉년에 윤달.meaning: ill often comes on the back of worse; one bad turn deserves another

호랑이도 제 말 하면 온다.meaning: speak of the devil (speak of the devil and he shall appear)

호사유피 인사유명.meaning: a person should focus on the legacy they will have on others

새해를 축하합니다.meaning: an expression wishing one the best in a new year; Happy New Year

소나기는 피하는 게 상책.meaning: "a rain shower is better off avoiding": don't pointlessly ask for trouble

어찌할 바를 모르다.meaning: be at a loss what to do

물 만난 고기.meaning: to find oneself in a suitable environment where one can perform well, up someone's alley, in one's element

못 본 체하다.meaning: to pretend not to see, to ignore, to overlook, to turn a blind eye

어쩔 수 없다.meaning: to be unavoidable, it can't be helped

신선놀음에 도낏자루 썩는 줄 모른다.meaning: time flies when you're having fun

틀린 그림 찾기.meaning: synonym of 다른 그림 찾기 (dareun geurim chatgi)

날로 먹다.meaning: to achieve or attain without effort; to free ride

소시지 야채 볶음.meaning: stir-fried sausage and vegetables (a Korean dish consisting of sausage, vegetables, ketchup, syrup, and sometimes gochuj

안 봐도 비디오.meaning: [like] a video tape even without watching (For something to be immediately obvious or clear, as if being played back fro

처음 뵙겠습니다.meaning: pleased to meet you, how do you do?
